title: The Fall of the Third Reich and Hitler's Death

sections.heading: The Failed Assassination, July 1944
sections.text: Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg's bomb at Rastenburg on 20 July 1944 failed to kill Hitler. The attempt triggered brutal reprisals: approximately 7,000 arrested, nearly 5,000 executed, including senior military figures. Field Marshal Rommel was forced to commit suicide.

sections.heading: Retreat and Collapse, 1944–1945
sections.text: By late 1944 Germany faced catastrophic defeat on all fronts. The failure of the Ardennes offensive (December 1944) exhausted the last reserves. Soviet forces crossed the Vistula in January 1945; American and British forces crossed the Rhine in March. Hitler rejected all advice to negotiate or withdraw to a defensible position.

sections.heading: The Berlin Bunker
sections.text: Hitler moved to the Führerbunker beneath the Reich Chancellery garden in January 1945. On 16 April Soviet forces began their final assault on Berlin. Hitler refused to leave; he expected 'Army Detachment Steiner' to counterattack and relieve the city — Steiner had no forces to do so. On 22 April, Hitler learned the truth and acknowledged Germany had lost the war.

sections.heading: Death, 30 April 1945
sections.text: On 29 April 1945, Hitler married Eva Braun in a brief ceremony in the bunker. He dictated his political testament — blaming the Jews for the war and urging Germany to continue racial struggle — and his personal will. At approximately 3:30 PM on 30 April 1945, Hitler shot himself. Eva Braun took poison simultaneously. Their bodies were burned in the Chancellery garden per Hitler's instructions. Germany surrendered unconditionally on 8 May 1945.
